title: Conduit architecture artifact
description: A .conduit/architecture.json (or the .conduit/architecture.proposed.json an agent writes for review). A versioned envelope wrapping an ArchDoc — a tree of graphs the app renders as a drill-down canvas. Beyond this schema, the app enforces three referential rules it validates in code (see the skill): graphs[rootGraph] MUST exist or the whole doc is discarded; every edge's source and target MUST be node ids in the SAME graph or the edge is dropped; a node's childGraph MUST be a key in graphs or it is cleared.

updatedAt.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last write. Set to Date.now().

archKind.description: The element's type — drives its color and icon. Any other value silently degrades to "service".

id.description: STABLE, unique id. Never change it across rewrites — edges and the review diff anchor to it.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line qualifier under the title.

description.description: Optional prose. THIS is the field an agent writes narrative/notes into.

x.description: Canvas x coordinate (px). OPTIONAL — omit it; Conduit auto-lays-out unpositioned graphs.

y.description: Canvas y coordinate (px). OPTIONAL — omit it; Conduit auto-lays-out unpositioned graphs.

childGraph.description: Optional id of the graph this node drills into. MUST be a key in the top-level graphs map or the app clears it.

inputs.description: Ordered input ports (left edge). What this component consumes.

outputs.description: Ordered output ports (right edge). What this component exposes/exports.

icon.description: Optional glyph key; unset falls back to the kind's default icon.

source.description: Id of the source node in this graph, OR the literal "boundary:in" (the parent component's inputs, inside a child graph).

target.description: Id of the target node in this graph, OR the literal "boundary:out" (the parent component's outputs, inside a child graph).

sourcePort.description: Optional: an OUTPUT Port.id on the source node (or a parent input port id when source is "boundary:in"). Omit for a legacy whole-node edge. An edge naming a port that doesn't exist is dropped.

targetPort.description: Optional: an INPUT Port.id on the target node (or a parent output port id when target is "boundary:out").

typeRef.description: A port/field type. Either a primitive, a list of another type, or a reference to a named interface in doc.interfaces.

interfaceId.description: Key into doc.interfaces. A ref to a missing interface clears (port → untyped; interface field → primitive any).

id.description: STABLE, unique within the node (across inputs+outputs). Edges reference it.

name.description: The import/export label the agent reads ("userId", "profile").

id.description: Graph id. By convention equals its key in the graphs map.

rootGraph.description: Key of the top-level graph in `graphs`. MUST exist there or the whole document is rejected.

graphs.description: All graphs by id. A node's childGraph points at another entry here for drill-down.

interfaces.description: Document-level registry of named structured types, keyed by id. A port or field references one via { kind: "ref", interfaceId }.
